sentences.sentence: More than a million people are likely to be disappointed by their experience of the Government's attempts to improve the democratic process.
sentences.translation: 一百多万人可能会对政府改善民主程序的尝试所带来的体验感到失望。

structure.text: are likely to be disappointed by
their experience of the Government's attempts

difficult_points: be likely to 表示较大可能性；be disappointed by 是被动式情感表达。
to improve... 修饰 attempts，说明政府尝试的目标。

expressions.text: be likely to be disappointed by

sentences.sentence: They may have signed an online petition against road pricing, but ministers are determined to push ahead with plans to make it more expensive to drive.
sentences.translation: 他们或许签署了反对道路收费的网上请愿书，但部长们决意继续推进提高驾车成本的计划。

structure.text: They may have signed an online petition against road pricing
to make it more expensive to drive

difficult_points: may have signed 表示对已发生行为的推测；but 引出政府立场的对比。
make it + 形容词 + to do 中 it 是形式宾语，to drive 是真正宾语。

sentences.sentence: The Government is convinced that this is the only way to reduce congestion and the environmental damage caused by motoring.
sentences.translation: 政府确信，这是减少拥堵和机动车出行所造成环境破坏的唯一办法。

structure.text: that this is the only way
to reduce congestion and the environmental damage

difficult_points: that 从句说明政府确信的内容；this 回指提高驾车成本。
caused by motoring 修饰 environmental damage，不修饰 congestion。

sentences.sentence: Why wait until you are forced off the road by costly charges?
sentences.translation: 何必等到高昂收费逼得你无法再开车呢？

structure.text: until you are forced off the road by costly charges

difficult_points: Why wait 是省略主语和助动词的反问，相当于 Why should you wait。
are forced off the road 是被动语态，表示因成本而被迫停驾。

sentences.sentence: You may enjoy the convenience of your car, but the truth is that for huge numbers of people, owning a car makes little financial sense.
sentences.translation: 你或许享受汽车带来的便利，但事实是，对大量人而言，拥有汽车在经济上并不划算。

structure.text: You may enjoy the convenience of your car
that for huge numbers of people, owning a car makes little financial sense

difficult_points: but 对比便利性与经济现实；that 引导表语从句。
owning a car 是动名词主语；make little financial sense 表示经济上不合理。

sentences.sentence: You'd be far better off giving it up and relying on other forms of transport."I'm 47 and I've never owned a car, despite having a job that requires me to travel all over the South-East to visit clients," says Donnachadh McCarthy, an environmental expert who specialises in advising people how to be greener.
sentences.translation: 放弃汽车、改用其他交通方式会让你的经济状况好得多。“我47岁，从未拥有过汽车，尽管我的工作要求我跑遍英格兰东南部拜访客户。”环境专家唐纳查德·麦卡锡说；他专门指导人们如何过得更环保。

structure.text: You'd be far better off giving it up and relying on other forms of transport
I'm 47 and I've never owned a car
despite having a job that requires me to travel all over the South-East to visit clients
an environmental expert who specialises in advising people how to be greener

difficult_points: be better off doing 表示做某事后处境更好；giving 与 relying 并列。
源文句号后紧接开引号、没有空格，sentence 中已保留。
that 修饰 job；who 修饰 expert，形成不同层级的定语从句。

expressions.text: rely on other forms of transport

sentences.sentence: "A car is a huge financial commitment, as well as being a psychological addiction.
sentences.translation: “汽车是一项巨大的经济负担，同时也会造成心理依赖。

structure.text: as well as being a psychological addiction

difficult_points: financial commitment 指持续承担的大笔开支，不只是一次购车款。
as well as 后接 being，补充汽车还会形成心理依赖；引语未在本句闭合。

sentences.sentence: Not owning a vehicle is far more practical than most people realise."
sentences.translation: 不拥有汽车其实比大多数人意识到的要实际得多。”
difficult_points: Not owning... 是否定动名词短语作主语。
than 从句中 realise 后省略 not owning a vehicle is practical。

sentences.sentence: It may seem as if cars have never been cheaper.
sentences.translation: 看起来，汽车似乎从未像现在这样便宜。

structure.text: as if cars have never been cheaper

difficult_points: as if 引导表语性从句，描述表面印象。
have never been cheaper 用比较级表达“达到历史最低价”。

sentences.sentence: After all, it is now possible to buy a brand new car for less than £4,800 - the Perodua Kelisa, if you're interested.
sentences.translation: 毕竟，现在不到4800英镑就能买到一辆全新汽车——如果你感兴趣，就是 Perodua Kelisa。

structure.text: to buy a brand new car for less than £4,800

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正主语是 to buy...。
短横线后给出车型实例，if you're interested 是对读者的插入话语。

sentences.sentence: There are plenty of decent vehicles you can buy straight from the showroom for between £5,000 and £7,000.
sentences.translation: 有许多品质不错的汽车可以直接从展厅购买，价格在5000至7000英镑之间。

structure.text: you can buy straight from the showroom

difficult_points: vehicles 后省略关系词 that，you can buy... 修饰它。
for 引出售价，between... and... 表示价格区间。

sentences.sentence: Of course, if you buy second-hand, the prices will be even lower.
sentences.translation: 当然，如果购买二手车，价格还会更低。
difficult_points: buy second-hand 中 second-hand 作方式补语，表示购买二手商品。
even 修饰比较级 lower，强调价格进一步下降。

sentences.sentence: However, the falling purchase price of cars masks the fact that it has never been more expensive to own and run a vehicle.
sentences.translation: 然而，汽车购买价格下降掩盖了一个事实：拥有和使用汽车的成本从未如此高昂。

structure.text: the falling purchase price of cars
that it has never been more expensive to own and run a vehicle

difficult_points: that 同位语从句说明 fact 的具体内容。
it 是形式主语，to own and run... 是真正主语；never more expensive 表示成本达到历史高位。

sentences.sentence: The estimate is that the cost of running a car rose by more than ten per cent last year alone.
sentences.translation: 据估计，仅去年一年，汽车使用成本就上涨了10%以上。

structure.text: that the cost of running a car rose by more than ten per cent last year alone

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，给出 estimate 的具体数字。
rose by 表示上涨幅度；last year alone 强调仅一年内的变化。

expressions.text: the cost of running a car

sentences.sentence: The annual cost of running your own vehicle is pul at an average of £5,539, or £107 a week.
sentences.translation: 自有汽车的年度使用成本估计平均为5539英镑，即每周107英镑。

structure.text: The annual cost of running your own vehicle

difficult_points: 源文 is pul at 疑为 is put at，表示估算为某数值，但必须原样保留。
or 后用每周数字换算说明年度平均成本。

sentences.sentence: While drivers who do less or more than the average mileage each year will spend correspondingly less or more, many of the costs of ear ownership are fixed - and therefore unavoidable.
sentences.translation: 虽然每年行驶里程低于或高于平均水平的司机会相应少花或多花一些，但汽车所有权的许多成本是固定的，因此无法避免。

structure.text: who do less or more than the average mileage each year
will spend correspondingly less or more
many of the costs of ear ownership

difficult_points: While 引导让步从句，who 修饰 drivers；less or more 两组表达前后呼应。
源文 ear ownership 疑为 car ownership，已原样保留；短横线后的形容词补充结果。

sentences.sentence: Depreciation - the fact that your vehicle loses a large chunk of its resale value each year - is one problem, accounting for £2,420 a year.
sentences.translation: 折旧——即汽车每年损失大部分转售价值——是一个问题，每年要占去2420英镑。

structure.text: the fact that your vehicle loses a large chunk of its resale value each year

difficult_points: 两个短横线之间是 Depreciation 的同位解释，其中 that 为 fact 的同位语从句。
account for 在此表示成本占到某个金额。

expressions.text: lose a large chunk of its resale value

sentences.sentence: The cost of finance packages, which most people have to resort to to pay for at least part of the price of a new car, has also been rising - to an average of £1,040 a year.
sentences.translation: 融资方案的成本也一直上涨，平均达到每年1040英镑；大多数人不得不借助此类方案支付至少部分新车价款。

structure.text: which most people have to resort to to pay for at least part of the price of a new car
to an average of £1,040 a year

difficult_points: which 回指 finance packages，在从句中作 resort to 的宾语。
连续两个 to 中，第一个属于 resort to，第二个引出目的不定式 to pay。

expressions.text: at least part of the price

sentences.sentence: Then there's insurance, maintenance, tax, and breakdown insurance, all of which will cost you broadly the same amount, however many miles you do.
sentences.translation: 此外还有保险、保养、税费和故障保险；无论行驶多少英里，这些项目的花费大体相同。

structure.text: Then there's insurance, maintenance, tax, and breakdown insurance
all of which will cost you broadly the same amount

difficult_points: all of which 回指前面列出的四类费用。
however many miles 表示无论多少里程，do 后省略 drive。

sentences.sentence: Only fuel costs are truly variable.
sentences.translation: 只有燃油成本是真正可变的。
difficult_points: Only 修饰主语 fuel costs，因此不触发倒装。
variable 与前文 fixed costs 构成直接对比。

sentences.sentence: While petrol prices are the most visible indicator of the cost of running a car, for the typical driver they account for less than one fifth of the real costs each year.
sentences.translation: 虽然汽油价格是汽车使用成本中最显眼的指标，但对普通司机而言，它每年只占实际成本的不到五分之一。

structure.text: While petrol prices are the most visible indicator of the cost of running a car
account for less than one fifth of the real costs each year

difficult_points: While 引导让步，表面可见程度与实际占比形成反差。
they 回指 petrol prices；account for 表示占总成本比例。

sentences.sentence: In other words, leaving aside all the practical and psychological barriers to giving up your car, in financial terms, doing so makes sense for many people.
sentences.translation: 换言之，先不考虑放弃汽车所面临的实际和心理障碍，仅从经济角度看，这样做对许多人是合理的。

structure.text: leaving aside all the practical and psychological barriers to giving up your car

difficult_points: leaving aside... 表示暂不纳入考虑；barriers to 中 to 是介词，后接 giving up。
doing so 回指 giving up your car；in financial terms 限定结论角度。

sentences.sentence: Take the cost of public transport, for example.
sentences.translation: 以公共交通成本为例。
difficult_points: Take... for example 是祈使式举例结构，主语 you 省略。
the cost of public transport 是接下来用于比较的对象。

sentences.sentence: In London, the most expensive city in the UK, the most expensive annual travel card, allowing travel in any zone at any time, costs just over £1,700.
sentences.translation: 在英国生活成本最高的城市伦敦，最贵的年度交通卡可在任意时间乘车前往任意区域，价格也只略高于1700英镑。

structure.text: the most expensive city in the UK
the most expensive annual travel card
allowing travel in any zone at any time

difficult_points: the most expensive city... 同位解释 London；第二个 most expensive 修饰 travel card。
allowing... 修饰交通卡，说明其使用范围。

sentences.sentence: You could give up your car and still have thousands of pounds to spare to spend on occasional car hire.
sentences.translation: 你可以放弃汽车，仍能省下数千英镑，用于偶尔租车。

structure.text: and still have thousands of pounds to spare
to spend on occasional car hire

difficult_points: give up 与 have 共用主语；still 强调弃车后仍有余钱。
to spare 表示可剩余支配，后一个 to spend 说明用途。

expressions.text: have thousands of pounds to spare

sentences.sentence: In fact, assuming that you have the most expensive travel card in London, you could hire a cheap car from a company, such as easyCar for about 30 weeks a year, and still be better off overall than if you own your own vehicle.
sentences.translation: 事实上，即使假定你持有伦敦最昂贵的交通卡，一年再从 easyCar 等公司租用廉价汽车约30周，总体经济状况仍会好于自己拥有汽车。

structure.text: assuming that you have the most expensive travel card in London
could hire a cheap car from a company
such as easyCar for about 30 weeks a year
and still be better off overall than if you own your own vehicle

difficult_points: assuming that... 是分词条件状语，表示按最昂贵情形计算。
such as easyCar 修饰 company；than if... 引出与自有车辆的假设比较。

sentences.sentence: Not that car hire is necessarily the most cost-effective option for people who are prepared to do without a car but may still need to drive occasionally.
sentences.translation: 这并不是说，对于愿意不拥有汽车、但偶尔仍需驾车的人而言，租车一定是最具成本效益的选择。

structure.text: Not that car hire is necessarily the most cost-effective option
who are prepared to do without a car
but may still need to drive occasionally

difficult_points: Not that... 是省略式免责声明，意为“并不是说……”。
who 修饰 people，并连接 are prepared 与 may need 两个谓语。

sentences.sentence: Streetcar, one of several "car clubs" with growing numbers of members, reckons that using its vehicles twice a week, every week, for a year, would cost you just £700.
sentences.translation: Streetcar 是会员数量不断增长的几家“汽车俱乐部”之一；它估算，每周使用其车辆两次、持续一年，只需花费700英镑。

structure.text: one of several "car clubs" with growing numbers of members
that using its vehicles twice a week, every week, for a year, would cost you just £700

difficult_points: one of... 是 Streetcar 的同位语；with... 修饰 car clubs。
that 从句以 using... 动名词短语作主语，would cost 是谓语。

sentences.sentence: Streetcar's model works very similarly to those of its main rivals, Citycarclub and Whizzgo.
sentences.translation: Streetcar 的运营模式与主要竞争对手 Citycarclub 和 Whizzgo 的模式非常相似。
difficult_points: those 代替复数 models，避免重复。
Citycarclub and Whizzgo 同位说明 rivals 的具体公司。

sentences.sentence: These three companies, which now operate in 20 of Britain's towns and cities, charge their members a refundable deposit - £150 at Streetcar - and then provide them with an electronic smart card.
sentences.translation: 这三家公司目前在英国20个城镇运营，会向会员收取可退还的押金——Streetcar 为150英镑——然后给他们一张电子智能卡。

structure.text: which now operate in 20 of Britain's towns and cities
charge their members a refundable deposit
and then provide them with an electronic smart card

difficult_points: which 补充公司的运营范围；charge 后接 members 与 deposit 双宾语。
provide them with... 表示向会员提供智能卡，them 回指 members。

sentences.sentence: This enables members to get into the vehicles, which are left parked in set locations, and the keys are then found in the glove compartment.
sentences.translation: 这使会员能够进入停放在固定地点的车辆，而钥匙就放在手套箱里。

structure.text: enables members to get into the vehicles
which are left parked in set locations
and the keys are then found in the glove compartment

difficult_points: This 指 electronic smart card；enable + 宾语 + to do 构成复合宾语。
which 修饰 vehicles；are left parked 表示车辆保持停放状态。

sentences.sentence: Members pay an hourly rate for the car - £4.95 is the cost at Streetcar - and return it to the same spot, or to a different designated parking place.
sentences.translation: 会员按小时支付用车费——Streetcar 每小时4.95英镑——然后把车还到原处或另一个指定停车点。

structure.text: pay an hourly rate for the car
and return it to the same spot, or to a different designated parking place

difficult_points: 短横线之间是完整插入句，给出 Streetcar 的具体费率。
return 的宾语 it 指 car，两个 to 短语并列说明还车地点。

sentences.sentence: Car sharing is an increasingly popular option for people making the same journeys regularly -to and from work, for example.
sentences.translation: 拼车日益成为定期走相同路线者的热门选择，例如上下班往返。

structure.text: for people making the same journeys regularly
to and from work, for example

difficult_points: making... 是现在分词修饰 people。
源文短横线紧接 to，没有常规空格，已保留。

sentences.sentence: Many companies run schemes that help colleagues who live near to each other and work in the same place to contact each other so they can share the journey to work.
sentences.translation: 许多公司开展项目，帮助住得近且在同一地点工作的同事彼此联系，以便共同通勤。

structure.text: who live near to each other and work in the same place
so they can share the journey to work

difficult_points: that 修饰 schemes，who 又修饰 colleagues，形成嵌套定语从句。
help colleagues to contact 是复合宾语；so 引出共同通勤的目的。

sentences.sentence: Liftshare and Carshare are two national organisations that maintain online databases of people who would be prepared to team up.
sentences.translation: Liftshare 和 Carshare 是两家全国性组织，维护着愿意结伴出行者的在线数据库。

structure.text: that maintain online databases of people
who would be prepared to team up

difficult_points: that 修饰 organisations，who 修饰 people，形成两层修饰。
be prepared to team up 表示愿意与他人结伴合作。

sentences.sentence: Other people may be able to replace part or all of their journey to work - or any journeys, for that matter - with low-cost transport such as a bicycle, or even by just walking.
sentences.translation: 另一些人或许能用自行车等低成本交通方式，甚至仅靠步行，取代部分或全部通勤路程——其实任何出行都可以如此。

structure.text: part or all of their journey to work
or any journeys, for that matter
with low-cost transport such as a bicycle, or even by just walking

difficult_points: replace A with B 表示用 B 替代 A；part or all 构成范围并列。
短横线内 for that matter 扩展到任何旅程；with transport 与 by walking 表达方式不完全平行但属原文。

sentences.sentence: The more you can reduce your car use, however you gain access to it, the more you will save.
sentences.translation: 无论你通过何种方式获得汽车使用权，减少用车越多，省下的钱就越多。

structure.text: The more you can reduce your car use
however you gain access to it

difficult_points: the more..., the more... 是关联比较结构，表示两个变化同步增长。
however 表示无论以何种方式；it 回指 car。
